general.hero_badge: SOC-Focused Cybersecurity Analyst | Blue Team Trainee
general.hero_headline: I help security teams detect faster, investigate smarter, and communicate clearly under pressure.
general.hero_summary: Computer Science student and cybersecurity trainee focused on SOC operations, incident response, log analysis, and network investigation. I bring hands-on lab practice plus an engineering mindset that helps me understand how systems behave, where they fail, and how to defend them better.
hero_points: Hands-on training in SIEM workflows, alert triage, phishing analysis, and incident response reporting.
Strong infrastructure and development background that translates into better troubleshooting and more security-aware thinking.
Ready for junior SOC, security monitoring, and blue-team entry roles with high ownership and fast learning speed.
general.availability: Open to SOC, IR, and Blue Team Roles
general.about_intro: This portfolio is intentionally positioned for cybersecurity hiring teams, not generic tech roles. It highlights the evidence that matters most for entry-level security work: investigation ability, technical context, structured thinking, and fast operational growth.
general.about_main: My strongest edge is that I do not approach security as isolated theory. I understand applications, authentication flows, network behavior, access control, and the operational side of troubleshooting. That gives me a more grounded way to analyze suspicious activity, reason about root causes, and write reports that engineers and analysts can actually use.
general.about_support: I am actively building toward SOC and incident response roles through DEPI, TryHackMe, LetsDefend, and continuous lab work. I care about being dependable during pressure, clear in communication, and disciplined in documentation, because those traits matter just as much as tool familiarity inside real security teams.
general.goal_text: Join a security team where I can contribute in Tier 1 SOC operations, incident triage, monitoring, and investigative reporting, then grow deeper into incident response and threat hunting.
general.contact_summary: If you are hiring for a junior SOC, security analyst, or incident response track role, I would love to connect. I am especially valuable in teams that want someone coachable, technically grounded, and serious about operational quality.
general.footer_copy: Mohamed Elhemaly - Cybersecurity portfolio focused on SOC operations, incident response, and blue-team growth.

snapshot_items.title: Security-First Positioning
snapshot_items.text: Everything here is tailored to show readiness for cybersecurity roles instead of splitting attention across unrelated tracks.

snapshot_items.title: Engineering Context
snapshot_items.text: Development and infrastructure experience are reframed as security advantages: better visibility into auth, logs, access, and systems behavior.

snapshot_items.title: Operational Communication
snapshot_items.text: I focus on structured findings, clean documentation, and incident summaries that help teams move from signal to action faster.

strengths.title: SOC Monitoring & Triage
strengths.description: Comfortable following the early incident lifecycle: reviewing alerts, checking context, spotting suspicious patterns, and escalating with structured notes and timelines.

strengths.title: Network Visibility
strengths.description: Able to reason about packet flow, connectivity issues, and suspicious traffic patterns using networking knowledge and hands-on analysis labs.

strengths.title: Investigation Discipline
strengths.description: I focus on evidence, timelines, documentation, and repeatable investigation steps instead of guessing. That makes my security work cleaner and easier to trust.

strengths.title: Application Security Awareness
strengths.description: Building admin panels, auth flows, and real-time systems gave me practical intuition around access control, risky logic, and secure implementation patterns.

strengths.title: Vulnerability Assessment Support
strengths.description: Hands-on familiarity with scanning, interpreting findings, and translating technical issues into practical remediation language inside controlled lab environments.

strengths.title: Fast-Learning Teammate
strengths.description: I learn quickly, communicate calmly, and take ownership. For junior security roles, that often matters as much as the current tool list.

projects.title: SOC Investigation Lab
projects.description: Worked through simulated SIEM alerts, correlated evidence, reconstructed an incident timeline, and documented the analyst decision path from detection to reporting.
projects.outcome: Demonstrated readiness for Tier 1 workflow habits: triage, evidence review, prioritization, and concise reporting.

projects.title: Web Vulnerability Assessment in Controlled Lab
projects.description: Performed reconnaissance and vulnerability review on a lab target using scanning and validation tools, then organized findings by severity and remediation priority.
projects.outcome: Strengthened my ability to convert raw scan output into a practical security story rather than a noisy tool dump.

projects.title: Incident Response Reporting Exercise
projects.description: Produced a structured incident response report for a DEPI training scenario covering detection method, scope, containment, eradication, and lessons learned.
projects.outcome: Showed that I can organize technical findings into a format useful for both analysts and decision-makers.

projects.title: Secure Access Mindset from Admin Panel Development
projects.description: While building an admin control panel, I worked directly with authentication, role-based access ideas, privileged workflows, and operational controls that map naturally to security thinking.
projects.outcome: This project strengthens my understanding of how misconfigurations and weak access design can become security issues in real systems.

projects.title: Real-Time WebSocket Infrastructure Prototype
projects.description: Built a real-time communication service in PHP using WebSockets, which sharpened my understanding of persistent connections, event-driven behavior, and monitoring needs in stateful systems.
projects.outcome: Useful security crossover for understanding long-lived sessions, unusual traffic behavior, and operational visibility in modern apps.

projects.title: Security-Focused Personal Portfolio
projects.description: Redesigned this portfolio to present a clear, recruiter-friendly cybersecurity narrative with stronger positioning, stronger visual quality, and stronger evidence alignment.
projects.outcome: Makes the case for security hiring faster by removing mixed messaging and highlighting blue-team readiness first.

highlights: Completed practical training around SOC processes, digital forensics fundamentals, and structured incident handling workflows.
Worked through analyst-style labs involving detection logic, evidence review, and incident reporting.
Strengthened understanding of security operations discipline, not just isolated technical tasks.
Actively progressing through hands-on labs for SOC Level 1, phishing analysis, network traffic review, and SIEM fundamentals.
Practiced identifying suspicious behavior, validating findings, and documenting analyst decisions clearly.
Built comfort with repeated investigative workflows instead of one-off challenge solving.
Supported LAN infrastructure setup and troubleshooting across multiple departments.
Improved my ability to analyze connectivity issues, network behavior, and operational dependencies.
Created technical notes and topology understanding that now strengthen my network-security perspective.
Built systems involving authentication, data handling, access workflows, and real-time communication patterns.
Used development work to sharpen secure design awareness and understand what defenders need to monitor in applications.
Developed a stronger bridge between engineering detail and defensive analysis.

certifications.title: Cisco - Introduction to Cybersecurity
certifications.description: Foundational cybersecurity concepts, threat landscape, and basic defensive best practices.

certifications.title: Fortinet NSE 1-3
certifications.description: Network security learning path covering security awareness, threat concepts, and Fortinet platform fundamentals.

certifications.title: Sprints x Microsoft - Cybersecurity Fundamentals
certifications.description: Structured cybersecurity fundamentals program with emphasis on practical understanding and defensive awareness.

certifications.title: DEPI - Incident Response Track
certifications.description: Security operations and incident response training delivered through the Digital Egypt Pioneers Initiative.

certifications.title: TryHackMe - SOC Level 1
certifications.description: Hands-on blue-team path covering SIEM, log analysis, threat detection, and SOC workflows.

certifications.title: CCNA Networking Concepts
certifications.description: Academic networking foundation supporting stronger traffic analysis, troubleshooting, and network defense reasoning.
